activity_payload.prompt_context: Need a retry wrapper for our API client that handles rate limits (429s) with exponential backoff. Should respect the Retry-After header when present.
activity_payload.ai_response_summary: Generated a decorator using tenacity with exponential backoff starting at 1s, max 60s. Parses Retry-After header and overrides the backoff delay when present. Includes jitter.
activity_payload.rationale: Backoff logic was fine but it wasn't handling the case where Retry-After returns an HTTP-date instead of seconds (RFC 7231 allows both). Added a check that tries int parse first, falls back to datetime parse. Also bumped max retries from 3 to 5 because our integration tests showed 3 wasn't enough during peak hours.
activity_payload.uncertainty_description: Not sure if we need to handle Retry-After as an HTTP-date in practice — none of our current upstream APIs use that format. But the RFC says it's valid and I'd rather not find out the hard way. Went with handling both.
alternatives_considered: Fixed backoff, no header parsing (rejected — we'd hammer the API during outages)
urllib3 built-in retry (not enough control over backoff curve, and we need per-retry logging)
